reasoning: The Lean formalization correctly captures each element of the informal statement. `[CommGroup G] [Fintype G]` encodes "finite abelian group"; `hp : Nat.Prime p`, `hp1 : ¬ p ∣ m`, and `hG : card G = p^n * m` match "order p^n·m with p prime and p ∤ m"; `[Fintype H]` with `hH : card H = p^n` matches "subgroup H of order p^n"; and `Subgroup.Characteristic H` in Mathlib is defined via `∀ ϕ : G ≃* G, H.comap ϕ.toMonoidHom = H`, which is exactly the standard definition of a characteristic subgroup (fixed by all automorphisms). The quantifier structure (G, p, m, n, H universally quantified in outer context) matches the informal reading. Edge cases like m = 0 are excluded automatically since a Fintype group has nonzero cardinality.
